paper_announcements.summary: Paper about SAM 3.1 with object multiplexing to improve video processing, released by Meta AI.
Research on OmniReset framework for training robot policies using large-scale RL and diverse resets for contact-rich tasks.
Paper on 'Self-Distillation of Hidden Layers for Self-Supervised Representation Learning' introducing Bootleg method.
Research showing AI sycophancy is prevalent and consequential when discussing interpersonal conflicts with AI systems.
Intern-S1-Pro: Scientific Multimodal Foundation Model at Trillion Scale paper released.
slime v0.2.4 release focusing on profiling and observability with rollout trace timeline viewer and real-time W&B reporting.
AI Eleuther community work investigating how subliminal prompting propagates across networks of interacting agents.
Agent data protocol dataset with 3M+ trajectories unifying different agent traces in a single format.

announcements.summary: Nintendo Switch 2 available for $399/$439 Mario Kart Bundle on Woot with choice of physical or digital copy.
Pokemon Showdown working on effectiveness icons feature.
Avatar Legends: The Fighting Game preorder trailer released, priced at $29.99/$49.99 and coming July 2nd.
Marathon game reveals Vandal Runner Shell built for hit-and-run gameplay with fast repositioning movement abilities.
NEEDY GIRL OVERDOSE coming to Crunchyroll in April, featuring OMGkawaiiAngel seeking fame.
Hermes Agent v0.5.0 released by Nous Research.
University of Washington astronomers discover rare evidence of two planets colliding 11,000 light-years away.
Weekly Shonen Jump new series color previews for issues #19-21, including works by creators of AGRAVITY BOYS and Gintama.

discussions.summary: Discussion about whether Gaussian splats should be considered 'AI' and the problems with using 'AI' to refer to heterogeneous technologies.
Commentary on intelligence as a conversion ratio with optimality bounds, not an unbounded scalar like height that people often misconceive.
Experience report on robotaxi performing well - drove 30 miles point to point with no mistakes, unlike Waymo it can drive freeways.
Reflection on ideal timeline: growing up in the 90s, discovering neural nets, scaling laws, and building artificial consciousness.
Skepticism about claims that we're months away from automating all white collar work, responding to a thread about AI limitations.
McMansion Hell author expressing frustration about New York Times repeatedly plagiarizing their work without attribution.
Thread about recursive self-improvement in AI coming in fits and starts like human research, with S-curves and waiting periods.
Analysis of how the US/Israel relationship affects Iran negotiations and the different goals each country has in the region.
